data.title: Wall Street Volatility Gauge Hits 4.5-Year High, Traders Lift Rate-Cut Bets on China Tariffs
data.description:
• The VIX index, the equities volatility measure known as Wall Street's "fear gauge," rose to its highest since October 2020 as U.S.-China trade tensions escalated.
  • Traders increased their interest-rate cut expectations to 116 basis points this year, up from 100 basis points prior to the news that China had imposed retaliatory tariffs on U.S. goods.
  • The bitcoin price fell 0.7% to $82,500, with its 30-day implied volatility reaching a two-week high of 54.6%.

data.title: Bitcoin Falls Back to $83K as China Announces 34% Tariffs on All U.S. Goods
data.description:
• China announced retaliatory tariffs on all goods, worsening risk sentiment during European hours.
  • Bitcoin's value dropped by $1,600 to $83,000 after an initial rise, with other cryptocurrencies also losing early gains.
  • Futures tied to the S&P 500 and Nasdaq fell over 2% amid escalating global trade tensions.

data.title: Nasdaq Composite Saw One of Its Worst Days Since 2000 While Bitcoin Held Steady
data.description:
• The Nasdaq Composite dropped almost 5.5% on Thursday, one of the worst single-day losses since 2000
  • Bitcoin ended the day up 0.7% and rose Friday to reclaim $84,000.
  • Analyst Caleb Franzen highlighted bitcoin’s resilience against the S&P 500, noting support from the 200-day moving average during a risk-off environment.

data.title: March Jobs Report a 'Heads I Win, Tails You Lose' Moment for Bitcoin Bulls
data.description:
• President Trump's announcement of tariffs on 180 nations has led markets to anticipate recession risks and potential Federal Reserve rate cuts.
  • Strong March NFP could be brushed off as backward-looking while a weak report could add to recession fears.
  • Bitcoin's price stability above March lows suggests seller fatigue, with implied volatility indicating a potential 3.4% price swing in the next 24 hours.

data.title: Filecoin's FIL Spikes 30% as South Korean Exchange Upbit Lists the Token
data.description:
• Filecoin's FIL token spiked by 30% after South Korean exchange Upbit started listing FIL trading pairs.
  • Tokens listed on South Korean exchanges often trade at a premium to prices on international exchanges.

data.title: Solana's SOL Could See Nearly 6% Price Swing as Whales Dump Coins Before U.S. Jobs Data
data.description:
• Solana's SOL token may experience a 6% price swing in the next 24 hours, Volmex's implied volatility index suggests.
  • Whales dumped coins early today.
  • U.S. job data could influence interest-rate cut expectations, potentially impacting cryptocurrency prices.

data.title: Coinbase Institutional Is Close to Offering XRP Futures
data.description:
• Coinbase Institutional has filed with the CFTC to offer futures contracts tied to Ripple's XRP token.
  • The XRP futures will be cash-settled, margined contracts, trading under the ticker XRL, and are expected to go live on April 21, 2025.
  • This product will be the second CFTC-regulated XRP futures after Bitnomial's launch in March.

data.title: BTC, ETH, XRP Set For a Near-Term Bounce as Attention Turns to Rate Cuts
data.description:
• Crypto markets experienced high volatility following U.S. tariff announcements, with major tokens initially surging and then slumping.
  • Investors moved large volumes of Bitcoin, ETH, and XRP into exchanges, indicating a readiness to sell amid economic uncertainty.
  • Markets are anticipating the U.S. non-farm payroll report, which could influence Federal Reserve rate cut expectations and impact crypto prices.

data.title: TON's Dramatic Volatility Signals Market Uncertainty
data.description:
• TON experienced a 15.5% price drop from $4.20 to $3.55, with 48-hour volatility reaching 21.3% annualized.
  • Toncoin has since rebounded, climbing 7% in the past 24 hours to $4.13, with venture capital firms now holding over $400 million in TON.
  • Major countries' trade disputes and geopolitical tensions continue to influence crypto markets, with bitcoin fluctuating around $82,000-$83,500.

data.title: Crypto-Friendly Prime Broker Hidden Road in Active Takeover Talks: Sources
data.description:
• Prime broker Hidden Road has received takeover interest from a crypto native firm, according to sources, with talks ongoing.
  • Investment banking firm FT Partners is advising Hidden Road, the people said.
